Page3 of 9 1. SCOPE: This specification covers the minimum technical requirements for design, engineering, manufacturing, testing, inspection and performance of low voltage current transformers used in the LV distribution system which is So lid earthed neutral with rated voltage 400/230V & rated frequency 50 HZ. 2. Applicable standards: Unless otherwise specified in this specification , the low voltage CTs should comply with the latest edition of IEC standard and should be designed, manufactured and tested in accordance with the applicable IEC standards as following table: Table (1) S. No Standard No. Description 1 IEC 61869-1 Instrument transformers general requirements 2 IEC 61869-2 Additional requirements for current transformers.

3. ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS: The performance of CTs should be guaranteed for the following environmental conditions, any differences in the guaranteed performance should be clearly set out in the offer:

Page4 of 9 Table (2) Minimum ambient temperature -5°c Maximum ambient temperature 45°c (50 °c as option). Maximum relative humidity 95%. Maximum altitude 1000 m 4. DESIGN AND CONSTRUCTION: 4.1. Rated Primary and Secondary Current of LV CTs a) The rated secondary current is 5A. b) The rated primary currents as given at table (3). 4.2. The secondary wires should be high-temperature insulated copper and not less than 2.5 mm² 4.3. The connection of the secondary wire and the windin g should be rigidly and permanently fixed, and the opening to where the secondary wire passes through should be provided with wire grommets or better to provide mechanical protection on the wire. 4.4. The LV CTs should be of ring - type /window-type without hinges and suitable

for horizontal and vertical installation. 4.5. The LV CTs should be provided with a suitable tin or silver plated electrolytic copper terminal block with purity not less than 99.99%. 4.6. It should be suitable for use in narrow switching cubicles an d suitable for threading over cables /bus bar size according to the following tables:

The C.T. should be sin gle phase, for metering purpose and should have the following technical data: • It should be insulated by cast resin or suitable insulation. • Maximum service voltage 600V. • C.T. Accuracy class 0.5S (class 0.2 Optional as per … EDC Requirements). • The rated short time thermal current (r.m.s) should be according to table (3, 4, and 5) with a minimum duration of 1 second. • Rated power-frequancy withstand voltage (r.m.s) for 1 min should be 3 KV. • Safety Factor for Measuring: FS5 (FS10 Optional as per … EDC Requirements)

• Rated continuous current: 1.2 I rated primary current. • Insulation class ≥ E (temperature rise limit “K” 70 °C @ 45°C ambient) and according to IEC 61869-2. • Rated dynamic current Ip (peak): 2.5x I thermal. 5. MARKING: The following data should be marked on CT itself or laser printed on the CT, and located in a place such that they are visible and legible when CT is installed: • The manufacturer's name/ model • The serial number and Year of Manufacturing • Class of insulation

Page7 of 9 • Symbol of ……EDC. • The rated primary and secondary current • The polarization on both sides. • The rated frequency • The rated output, Burden and corresponding accuracy class 6. SEALING: CT should be provided with suitable tool for sealing the secondary terminals of the current transformer. 7. TESTS: a. Type tests: - Temperature-rise test - Tests for accuracy - Short-Time current tests (short circuit test for the highest rate for each models (series)). Type tests should be in accordance with IEC 61869-1, 2. Type test certificates should be submitted with the offer. b. Routine/Acceptance Tests: - Power-frequency voltage withstand tests - Tests for accuracy - Verification of markings
- Turns ratio error. Acceptance tests should be performed on random samples and should be selected and witnessed by Distribution Company representative.

Page8 of 9 Routine tests should be performed in compliance with IEC61869-1,2 8. Sample: The tenderer should provide a sample C.T. with his offer. The sample should correspond in all respects with the material they intend to supply. 9. GUARANTEE: The tenderer should guarantee the CT against all defects arising out of faulty designed or workmanship or of defective materials for a period of 12 months from putting in service, or 18 months after delivery, t he good quality and manufacturing of the materials should be guaranteed. 10. GUARANTEE TABLES: Tenders not accompanied with attached table of guarantee fulfilled and signed by tenderers should not be considered. 11. SUBMITTALS: a) The tenderer should submit a complete set of dimensional drawings, technical

catalogues and user manual showing the CT type offered and its performance in full details. b) A copy of the latest applicable High Voltage Research Center Test Certificate should be attached with the tender , and the rest of the type tests must be completed from an accredited and neutral laboratory in accordance with IEC standards. c) A valid approved letter must be submitted from the Egyptian Electricity Holding Company attached to the submitted offer.

General Description: Insulated Current Transformers (CTs) are casted in epoxy resin and designed for rated voltages up to 24 kV. CTs are fabricated with required dimensions, single ratio or double ratios having the pos sibility to be re -connectable on the secondary side accord ing to …..EDC requirements. Page 5 DESIGN CRITERIA: • The current transformer should be designed according to latest edition of IEC standard publication IEC 61869-1, IEC 61869-2. • The primary current, secondary current and voltage ratings should be as specified above. • Short time current ratings of protective and metering current transformers should be equal to or higher than that of associated switchgear andequipment as applicable and should meet the above requirements. • Continuous thermal current for all CTs at highest tap should be as per above requirements without exceeding temperature limits. • Unless otherwise specified all current transformers CT core f or protection should have ratings and performance requirements of relaying including transient

performance characteristics at specified tap. • CTs should be designed to operate continuously for the respective application /installations. • Unless otherwise spec ified, the metering/protection class should be as per IEC at specified tap. • The current Tr. shall have terminal box for the secondary terminal and earth terminal. • The routine test certificate including magnetization curve for the core material should be submitted. • The current Tr. Should be interchangeable from lower ratio to higher ratio from the secondary side of the current Tr. • Maximum winding temperature rise should be according to IEC 61869-2. • Limits of current error and phase displacement should be in accordance with IEC

Page 3 Scope: This specification covers the minimum technical requirements for design, manufacturing, testing, packing and procurement MV voltage transformer used for measuring and protective relays at 50HZ. General description: Single-pole insulated voltage transformers casted in epoxy resin and designed for insulation voltages of 12 kV and 24 KV. The transformer is equipped with two secondary windings (can be three if required), the first one for measuring and the other for protection. The transformer can be mounted in any position. The secondary terminal board should be covered with a transparent and sealable cover made of suitable insulated material. In case of Voltage transformer equipped with fuse, the fuse link should be easy to be repl aced

(According to distribution company requirement). Unless otherwise specified, the transformers are manufactured conformably to the requirements and recommendations of IEC. Applicable standards: Unless otherwise specified, the voltage transformer should comply with the latest edition of IEC publications or any other equivalent international standard specification approved by (….EDC). ـ IEC 61869-1, General requirements ـ IEC 61869-3, Additional requirements for inductive voltage transformers ـ IEC 60282-1, high voltage fuses ـ And any relevant IEC standards :Environmental conditions The performance of the voltage transformer should be guaranteed for the following table, any differences in the guaranteed performance should be clearly set out in the offer:
Minimum ambient temperature -5°c Maximum ambient temperature 45°c (50 °c as option). Maximum relative humidity 95%. Maximum altitude 1000 m.

Page 3 of 26 1. SCOPE • This specification describes the general requirements governing the design, manufacturing and testing of the prefabricated high-voltage/low voltage substation with glass fiber reinforced polyester (GRP) housing up to 24 kV type tested according to IEC 62271-202 ed.2.0 standard. • The prefabricated high-voltage/low voltage substation is commonly called Compact Secondary Substation (CSS) and it can be called Package Substation or Package Distribution Substation. • The Compact Secondary Substation (CSS) comprises of an enclosure, transformer, Medium Voltage (MV) switchgear or Ring Main Unit (RMU), Low Voltage Switchboard (LVS), medium voltage and low voltage interconnections and auxiliary equipment to supply low voltage energy from a medium

voltage system or vice versa • The CSS enclosure expected operating lifetime is 30 years under the specified environmental, climatic, system and service conditions specified, with minimum maintenance requirements. • The power rating of the CSS will be defined as per the rating of the installed transformer so different power ratings of the CSS are available. 2. APPLICABLE CODES & STANDARDS The specifications shall be read in conjunction with the latest version of EDMS specifications Table (1) EDMS 2-100 12 KV AIR RING MAIN UNITS EDMS 2-102 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS FOR 12 KV RING MAIN UNIT ( GIS / AIS) EDMS 2-200 24 KV AIR RING MAIN UNITS EDMS 2-202 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS FOR 24 KV RING MAIN UNIT ( GIS / AIS)

EDMS 02-101 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR 12 KV AUTOMATED SMART RMU ( GIS / AIS) EDMS 02-103 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR 12 KV NON-MOTORIZED RING MAIN UNIT (GIS / AIS) EDMS 02-203 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR 24 KV NON-MOTORIZED RING MAIN UNIT (GIS / AIS) EDMS 02-201 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR 24 KV AUTOMATED SMART RMU ( GIS / AIS) EDMS 8-100 SPECIFICATIONS FOR OIL – IMMERSED DISTRIBUTION TRANSFORMER FROM 25 KVA TO 5000 KVA 10.5- 11/ 0.4 KV EDMS 8-200 SPECIFICATIONS FOR OIL – IMMERSED DISTRIBUTION TRANSFORMER FROM 25 KVA TO 5000 KVA 22/ 0.4 KV EDMS 8-101 SPECIFICATIONS FOR DRY TYPE TRANSFORMER FROM 50 KVA TO 5000 KVA 10.5- 11/ 0.4 KV EPOXY SOLID – CAST / CAST RESIN COIL EDMS 8-202 SPECIFICATIONS FOR DRY TYPE TRANSFORMER FROM 50 KVA TO 5000 KVA
22/ 0.4 KV EPOXY SOLID – CAST / CAST RESIN COIL EDMS 19-400 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR HIGH RUPTURE CAPACITY MV CURRENT LIMITING FUSES ( HRC) 12 KV & 24 KV

Page 4 of 26 The CSS enclosure, installed electrical and the relevant equipment shall be designed, manufactured and tested according to the latest version of IEC standards as per the below: Table (2) IEC 62271-202 High-voltage / low voltage prefabricated substations IEC/TR 62271-208 Electromagnetic fields generated by CSS IEC 60529 Degree of protection provided by an enclosure (IP code) IEC 62262 Degree of protection against external mechanical impacts (IK code) IEC 60721 Classification of environmental conditions 3. ENVIRONMENTAL AND SYSTEM CONDITIONS The substation is preferred to be used under field sites which may be exposed to severe weather and/or vandalism conditions for outdoor switchgear and control gear according to IEC 62271-1 ( Latest version)

Service conditions Table (3) Minimum ambient Temperature -5°C Ambient Temperature (max.) +45 °C (50 °C as option ) Relative humidity up to 95% Altitude (above sea level) up to 1000m 4. DESIGN CRITERIA: 1) The CSS shall be of Glass Reinforced Polyester (GRP) construction. This applies also to the roof, doors and ventilation louvres. 2) CSS shall facilitate the installation/ removal of any equipment (transformer- RMU – LV panel) the arrangement from the doors. 3) CSS shall be provided with lifting holes from bottom so that the whole unit can be lifted and positioned on concrete plinth at site by crane without causing any damage or distortion 4) The CSS and the installed components shall be designed and manufactured to operate properly and

withstand: − The climatic & environmental conditions stated above. − The electrical system parameters stated above. 5) The enclosure should be a robust design. The CSS enclosure shall operate safely in order to provide the needed level of safety and protection from un-authorized access. 6) Construction shall conform to IEC 62271-202:2014. This standard specifies the service conditions,

Page 5 of 26 rated characteristics, general structural requirements and test methods for prefabricated substations. 7) Each compartment shall have its own dedicated access and be segregated by sheet steel compartment walls with min. thickness of 2 mm for 11 KV (2.5 mm for 22 KV) (before painting) to protect an operator from a possible catastrophic failure of electrical apparatus in an adjacent compartment. 8) Each wall panel shall be constructed with a double layer GRP encapsulated an overall thickness of 50 mm and outer layer thickness not less than 4 mm. 9) The design of the CSS shall be based on safety to personnel and equipment during operation and maintenance. It shall be internal arc proof tested type (IAC-AB) to the arc current levels (IAC AB, 20

INSPECTION, AND TESTING: • Electricity Distribution Company (…EDC) reserves the right to witness the routine tests of the CSS. • Unless otherwise specified or approved in writing by …EDC, all kiosks should be tested at the supplier factory in accordance with the latest applicable issues of IEC standards. • Attendance of ...EDC representative during fabrication (assembly) stages, and testing should not relieve the supplier of his full responsibility for furnishing the kiosks in accordance of this specification requirements, and should not give him the right to invalidate any claim which …EDC, may make because of defective or unsatisfactory material or faulty workmanship. • All tests should be carried out at the supplier`s lap, and on his expense.

• The type test certificates should be submitted with offer to...EDC. • The tenderer should submit a quality control procedure, and approved letter with his offer. • In addition to the type tests conducted on individual components (Transformer, Ring Main Unit and Low voltage Switchboard), CSS as a whole unit shall be type tested in accordance with the requirements of IEC 62271-202 ed.2 and the copy of type test reports should be provided and issued from reputed independent laboratories. 10.1 CSS type tests: The CSS units shall be type tested in accordance with IEC standards 62271-202 and the following type tests have been performed and available if required: • Tests to verify the insulation levels of CSS (IEC 62271-202/6.2)

• Tests to prove the temperature rise of main components in CSS (IEC 62271-202/6.5) • Test to prove the capability of main and earthing circuits to be subjected to the rated peak and the rated short-time with stand currents (IEC 62271-202/6.6) • Tests to verify the degree of protection (IEC 62271-202/6.7) • Test to verify the withstand of the enclosure of the CSS against mechanical stress (IEC 62271 - 202/6.101) • For CSS substation class (IAC-AB @ 20kA-1 sec) tests to assess the effects of arcing due to an internal fault (IEC 62271-202/6.102) • Tests to verify the sound level of the CSS (IEC 62271-202/Annex-BB) 10.2. CSS routine tests The routine tests shall be made on each complete prefabricated substation or on each transport unit and,

whenever practicable, at the manufacturer's factory to ensure that the product is in accordance with the equipment on which the type test has been carried out. Each component shall be previously routine tested in accordance with their component standard, and for the assembly the following routine tests and verifications apply: • Dielectric test on MV interconnection (IEC 62271-202/7.101) • Test on auxiliary and control circuits (IEC 62271-202/7.102) • Functional tests (IEC 62271-202/7.103) • Verification of correct wiring (IEC 62271-202/7.104)

SPECIFICATIONS OF THE TRANSFORMER COMPARTMENT AIR TEMPERATURE CONTROLLER THROUGH CONTROLLING OPERATION OF TRANSFORMER COMPARTMENT VENTILATING FAN. THE REQUIRED IS: A thermostat of any suitable design complete with all accessories ( and a relay or contractor if the switching rating of the thermostat is low than the specified herein rating), and a

suitable prepared controller housing, in order to verify the following requirements, and specifications: 1- The purpose of the controller is to open the electrical circuit of the transformer compartment ventilating fan, (which is installed in a window highly positioned in one of the transformer Compartment walls) when the ventilating air temperature drops down to a certain adjustable value (between 30˚C, 80˚C), and closes this electric circuit when room air temperature rises 5˚C-8˚C above that adjust value. 2- System (fan supply) voltage: 220 (+10% volts, -15% volts) 3- Thermostat specifications: • Temperature range: 30˚C - 80˚C • Max working temperature at thermostat heat: 80˚C • Temperature differential (the difference between the opening, and closing temperature of the

thermostat): 5˚C - 8˚C • Manufacturing tolerance in opening, and closing temperature: low as possible. • Combined controller output contact rating (for fan operation): • Resistive: > 16 A • Inductive (full load ampere): > 5 A at p.f. 0.6 or 0.4 • The controller housing should be prepared for wall mounting.

SCOPE: • This specification covers the supply of integral pad -mounted distribution kiosks 11 kV, only (excluding the main power transformer). • These kiosks should be installed in networks where the medium voltage networks are unearthed, while the low voltage networks are solidly earthed. • Each kiosk should have three separate compartments: medium voltage compartment, transform er compartment, and low voltage compartment. 2. APPLICABLE STANDARDS • In addition to the requirements of this specification, the offered kiosk, and their components should comply with the latest applicable standards issued by the: IEC 62271-1 Common specification. for AC Switchgear, and control gear IEC 62271-200

AC metal -enclosed for switchgear, and control gear for rated voltages above 1kV, and up to, and including 52kV IEC 62271-202 High-voltage/Low-voltage prefabricated substation IEC 61439-1 Low voltage Switchgear, and control gear assemblies IEC- 60529 Classification of degree of protection provided by Enclosures 3. ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS • Minimum ambient temperature : -5°c. • Maximum ambient temperature : 45°C (50 °C as an option). • Maximum relative humidity : 95%. • Maximum altitude : 1000 m. 4. DESIGN CRITERIA: 1. The kiosk body, doors, and bottom should be of heavy-duty sheet steel at least (2.5 mm) thickness before any coating or painting. 2. The platform of the Tr. compartment should be of sheet steel of 4 mm thickness at least.

3. Possibility of removing transformer from the side of kiosk using a forklift. 4. The paint of the kiosk should be of electrostatic powder ( or stone paint as an option according to …EDC requirements) with thickness 60: 80 microns everywhere. 5. The kiosk should be internal arc proof (as an option according to…EDC requirements)

- 4 - 6. All the parts of kiosks should be Hot Dip Galvanized (HDG) (as an option according to…EDC requirements 7. The sheet steel of al l compartments, and unit chassis after all welding, and drilling have been completed should receive a suitable primary coat) of a rust -resistant paint at the end. The kiosk s should have an electrostatic painting of a grey color. 8. All the auxiliary connections should be executed using copper conductors (600V) fire-proof insulated stranded copper conductors of cross-sectional area at least (2.5 mm2) for current circuits, and (1.5 mm2) for voltage circuits. 9. The insula tion power frequency withst ands voltage for low voltage switchgear, and secondary circuits should be according to the latest IEC.

10. The roof of the kiosk should have a slope (not less than 6 degrees) to prevent the accumulation of rainwater. 11. The roof of the kiosk should have a suitable shape, and hooks for opening the roof. 12. The roof of the kiosk should be (HDG), and finished with electrostatic paint. 13. The base of the kiosk should be provided with a steel structure for lifting or hanging. 14. The kiosk should be totally enclosed, and weather-proof since it should be located in the open air. 15. The kiosk doors sh ould be provide d with suitable gaskets, and should have locks with a master key. 16. Except earthing copper bus bars, all main medium voltage bus bars should be fully insulated with anti-track heat-shrinkable tubes of voltage (12/20) kV, and all low voltage bus bars should be

insulated with colored heat-shrinkable tubes (red, yellow, blue, and black) of voltage (0.6/1) KV 17. The degree of protection should be at least (IP 23) for the transformer compartment, and (IP 54) for the medium voltage, and low voltage compartment. 18. الجزء العلوى للكشك على الجانب الايمن للوحة الجهد المنخفض او يتم تركيب المكثف الثابت داخل الكشك ( وذلك طبقا لطلب شركة التوزيع). 29- دخول كابلات المكثف الى لوحة الجهد المنخفض يلزم أن يكون من خلال جلاند على ان تكون درجة الحماية لاتقل عن IP54 ، و يلزم تأرض المكثف 30- يلزم ان يكون القاطعFORM 2B 31- يلزم لصق تعليمات تشغيل السكينة ( LBS) على الباب وكذلك اتجاه فتح وتغلق السكينة 32- بالنسبة لسكينة المحول يلزم لصق بالإضافة الى تعليمات التشغيل ( لا تقم بإعادة غلق السكينة الا بعد التأكدمن الراية الخاصة بفصل المحول بغرفة الجهد المنخفض فاذا كانت الراية تشير الى عطل يلزم فحص المحول والتأكد من سلامته أولا قبل التوصيل ) 33- يلزم لصق تعليمات على مراية القاطع الرئيسي (اذ وجدت القاطع في وضعtrip لا تعيد توصيل القاطع قبل التحقق من

وضع الراية الخاصة بفصل القاطع ) 34- يجب على الشركة المصنعة وضع لوحة معدنية على واجهة الكشك من ناحية الجهد المنخفض مدون بها الاتى: سنة الصنع الشركة المصنعة الرقم المسلسل للمحول كشك لمحول قدرة ( ك.ف.أ ) جهد تشغيل ك.ف رقم أمر التوريد / السنة EDC العميل

SPECIFICATIONS OF THE TRANSFORMER ROOM AIR TEMPERATURE CONTROLLER THROUGH CONTROLLING OPERATION OF ROOM VENTILATING FAN. THE REQUIRED IS: A thermostat of any suitable design complete with all accessories ( , and a relay or contractor if the switching rating of the thermostat is low than the specified herein rating ), and a suitable prepared controller housing, in order to ve rify the following requirements, and specifications: 1- The purpose of the controller is to open the electrical circuit of the transformer room ventilating fan, (which is installed in a window highly positioned in one of the transformer room walls)

when the v entilating air temperature drops down to a certain adjustable value (between 30˚C, 80˚C), and closes this electric circuit when room air temperature rises 5˚C-8˚C above that adjust value. 2- The transformers in the rooms are medium / low voltage distribution transformers of ratings up to 1600 KV.A. 3- System (fan supply) voltage: 220 (+10% volts, -15% volts) 4- Thermostat specifications: • Temperature range: 30˚C - 80˚C

-17- • Max working temperature at thermostat heat: 80˚C • Temperature differential (the difference between the opening, and closing temperature of the thermostat): 5˚C - 8˚C. • Manufacturing tolerance in opening, and closing temperature: low as possible. • Combined controller output contact rating (for fan operation): • Resistive: > 16 A • Inductive (full load ampere): > 5 A at p.f. 0.6 or 0.4 5- The controller housing should be prepared for wall mounting. 20. المواصفات الفنية لتوريد وتركيب نظـام إطفـاء تلقـائى بإستخدام الخرطوم الحساس بجهـاز إطـفاء ثانى أكسيد الكربون. الإستــخدام : يتتتتتتم إستتتتتتخدام ذلتتتتتك النظتتتتتـام المعتمتتتتتد علتتتتتى ختتتتتراطيم الإستشتتتتتعار الحساستتتتتة وذلتتتتتك لتغطيتتتتتة الأمتتتتتـاكن الضتتتتتيقة

والتى يصعب الوصول إليها مكــونات النظــام : 1. جهـاز إطـفاء بغاز ثانى أكسيد الكربون سعة10 كجم . 2. وحدة رأس جهاز الإطفـاء مزودة بمانوميتر 3. خرطوم الإستشعار 4. طـبه مناسبه للخرطوم 5. وسيلة مناسبه ومحكمه لتعليق بدن جهاز الإطفـاء . جهــاز الإطفــاء : 6. يكون بدن الجهاز من الصلب المسحوب على البارد ومطابقاً للمواصفات القياسية المصرية (م.ق.م 780 ) وأن يتحمل ضغط التفجير≥400 بار وضغط اختبار هيدروليكى≥200 بار 7. يكون التصنيع طبقاً للمواصفات القياسية المصرية 735 لسنة 2006 . 8. يجب أن يحتوى البدن على وسيلة لتعليق الجهاز بواسطة حامل يكفل له التثبيت بطريقة مأمونة إلى الحائط أو إلى الأرض 9. لا يحتوى البدن على فتحات بخلاف فتحة مجموعة الرأس فقط وألا يقل قطرها الداخلى عن19 مم 10. ألا يقل سمك البدن عند القمة والقاع عن6مم

11. أن يكون البدن مدهون من الخارج بدهان الكتروستاتيك بالأحمر اللامع ، ومعالج من الداخل ضد الصدأ ( فسفته) . 12. أن يحقق التفريغ للمحتويات90% بدون تجميد 13. يجب أن يعالج بدن الجهاز بالوسائل المناسبة والكفيلة بإزالة أية زيت أو شحوم أو مواد عازلة قبل الطلاء وبما يوفر درجة عاليه من مقاومة الصدأ والتماسك بطبقة الطلاء والتى يجب أن يتوفر فيها الجودة العالية ومقاومة العوامل الجوية والخدوش والصدمات

-18- 14. أن يكون الجهاز قد اجتاز جميع الاختبارات المنصوص عليها في المواصفة القياسية المصرية735/2006) 15. أن يوضح على الجهاز البيانات الآتية بشكلواضح يصعب محوه :- • إسم الصانع وعلامته التجارية وعنوانه والسجل التجاري والصناعي • جهاز ثاني أكسيد الكربون سعة-- ) كجم وتشمل كمية الغاز الأسميه ونوع الملأ. • طريقة إستعمال الجهاز (مختصرة وواضحة • عبارة ( تعاد تعبئة الجهاز بعد الاستعمال مباشرة • تاريخ صنع الجهاز و الرقم المسلسل للجهاز. • رقم المواصفة المصنع عليها الجهاز و وزن الجهاز فارغاً بمشتملاته ووزن الجهاز معبأ خرطوم الإسـتشعار : 1. خرطوم مصنوع من مـادة بوليمارية عـالية الجـودة . 2. ذات مرونة عـالية. 3. حـساس لدرجة الحرارة وينفجر الخرطوم عند أعلى نقطة تأثراً من 80 :100 درجة 4. عند الإنفـجار تتشكل فتحة الخرطوم كـأنها مخرج لنشر المـاده الخـاصه بالإطفـاء

5. لا يتــفاعل مع المواد الكيميائية 6. أن يكون القطـر الخـارجى للخرطوم6 مم والقطـر الداخلى4 مم 7. ضغط التشغيل20 بار 8. وحدة رأس جهاز الإطفـاء 9. تصمم مجموعة الرأس منالنحاس المسبوك تحت ضغط والمطلى بطبقه من النيكل كروم. 10. أن تكون الرأس معتمده طبقاً للمواصفات الأوروبية 11. تصمم مجموعة الرأس بحيث تشكل آلية جيدة للتحكم في التشغيل والإيقاف وتكون مأمونة 12. مزوده بصمام أمان لتصريف الشحنة الزائدة من الغاز عند ضغط يتراوح من183 إلي 200 كجم/سم2 . 13. شروط عامة لنظام الاطفاء: 14. أن يتم التنفيذ وفقاً لأصول الصـناعة 15. أن يتم تثبيت خرطوم الإستشعار بطريقه فنية مناسبة 16. يجب تقديم شهادة ضمان ضد عيوب الصناعة 17. يجب تقديم شهادة من الهيئة المصرية العامة للمواصفات والجودة ( التوحيدالقياسي ) أو شهادة موثقة من المصنع الرئيسي
عند الاستلام. 18. يجب تقديم شهادة منشأ أو شهادة من هيئة الرقابة على الصادرات والواردات عند الاستلام.

SCOPE: • This specification covers the supply of integral pad -mounted distribution kiosks 22 kV, only (excluding the main power transformer). • These kiosks should be installed in networks where the medium voltage networks are unearthed, while the low voltage networks are solidly earthed. • Each kiosk should have three separate compartments: medium voltage compartment, transformer compartment, and low voltage compartment. 2. APPLICABLE STANDARDS • In addition to the requirements of this specification, the offered kiosk, and their components should comply with the latest applicable standards issued by the: IEC 62271-1 Common specification. for AC Switchgear, and control gear IEC 62271-200

AC metal -enclosed for switchgear, and control gear for rated voltages above 1kV, and up to, and including 52kV IEC 62271-202 High-voltage/Low-voltage prefabricated substation IEC 61439-1 Low voltage Switchgear, and control gear assemblies IEC- 60529 Classification of degree of protection provided by Enclosures 3. ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS • Minimum ambient temperature : -5°c. • Maximum ambient temperature : 45°C (50 °C as an option). • Maximum relative humidity : 95%. • Maximum altitude : 1000 m. 4. DESIGN CRITERIA: 1. The kiosk body, doors, and bottom should be of heavy-duty sheet steel at least (2.5 mm) thickness before any coating or painting. 2. The platform of the Tr. compartment should be of sheet steel of 4 mm thickness at least.

3. Possibility of removing transformer from the side of kiosk using a forklift. 4. The paint of the kiosk should be of electr ostatic powder (or stone paint as an option according to …EDC requirements) with thickness 60: 80 microns everywhere. 5. The kiosk should be internal arc proof (as an option according to…EDC requirements)

- 4 - 6. All the parts of kiosk should be hot dip galvanized (HDG) ( as an option according to…EDC requirements 7. The sheet steel of al l compartments, and unit chassis after all welding, and drilling have been completed should receive a suitable primary coat) of a rust -resistant paint at the end. The kiosk s should have an electrostatic painting of a grey color. 8. All the auxiliary connections should be executed using copper conductors (600V) fire-proof insulated str anded copper conductors of cross-sectional area at least (2.5 mm2) for current circuits, and (1.5 mm2) for voltage circuits. 9. The insula tion power frequency withst ands voltage for low voltage switchgear, and secondary circuits should be according to the latest IEC.

10. The roof of the kiosk should have a slope (not less than 6 degrees) to prevent the accumulation of rainwater. 11. The roof of the kiosk should have a suitable shape, and hooks for opening the roof. 12. The roof of the kiosk should be (HDG) finished with electrostatic paint. 13. The base of the kiosk should be provided with a steel structure for lifting or hanging. 14. The kiosk should be totally enclosed, and weather-proof since it should be located in the open air. 15. The kiosk doors sh ould be provide d with suitable gaskets, and should have locks with a master key. 16. Except earthing copper bus bars, all main medium voltage bus bars should be fully insulated with anti-track heat-shrinkable tubes of voltage ( 18/30) kV, and all low voltage bus bars should be

insulated with colored heat-shrinkable tubes (red, yellow, blue, and black) of voltage (0.6/1) KV 17. The degree of protect ion should be at least (IP 23) for the transformer compartment, and (IP 54) for the medium voltage, and low voltage compartment. 18. The cable connecting the fused load-break switch to the transformer should be XLPE insulated with a copper conductor of cross-section area not less than 3x1x35 mm², 18/30 KV for transformers up to 500 KVA, and 3x1x70 mm²,18/30 KV for transformers more than 500 KVA, all with screen copper tapes [or tape & wires]. 19. The cable should be terminated with heat-shrinkable terminations or equivalent 18/30 KV. 20. All live parts of the medium, and low voltage compartments should be protected to a sufficient
degree of protection against contact with them during works of operation, inspection..............etc. 21. The medium voltage compartments should have a mimic diagram.

- 5 - 22. Each compartment should have its door which is equipped with a device that prevents self- locking when opened. 23. The outer enclosure of the kiosk should consist of heavy-duty structural steel frame-work of a substantial section, and dimensions to carry safely all the items to be mounted therein, the steel sections should be welded or riveted together. 24. The Kiosk should be provided with a suitable way to connect a security padlock on outside doors 25. Each load break switch compartment should be equipped with a suitable clamp or any other means for supporting when fixing the cables or end boxes. 26. Suitable inspection windows covered with hard transparent material should be provided as they

are necessary. 27. The load-break switch is preferred to be the rotatory type which is less affected by the dust. 28. Each load break switch should have a separate door, each door should have a rubber-sealing gasket. 29. The load break switches should be h, and-operated through levers. Their opening, and closing should be quick-acting due to energy stored in the relevant springs. 30. The medium voltage compartment (RMU is 2+1) should have a suitable space for one additional cable load break switch to be mounted in the future if required. 31. SPECIFICATIONS OF THE TRANSFORMER ROOM AIR TEMPERATURE CONTROLLER THROUGH CONTROLLING OPERATION OF ROOM VENTILATING FAN. THE REQUIRED IS: A thermostat of any suitable design complete with all accessories (, and a relay or contractor if the switching rating of the thermostat is low than the specified herein rating), and a suitable prepared controller housing, in order to ver ify the following requirements, and specifications: 1- The purpose of the controller is to open the electrical circuit of the transformer room ventilating

fan, (which is installed in a window highly positioned in one of the transformer room walls) when the ventilating air temperature drops down to a certain adjustable value (between 30˚C, 80˚C), and closes this electric circuit when room air temperature rises 5˚C -8˚C above that adjust value. 2- The transformers in the rooms are medium / low voltage distribution transformers of ratings up to 1600 KV.A. 3- System (fan supply) voltage: 220 (+10% volts, -15% volts) 4- Thermostat specifications: • Temperature range: 30˚C - 80˚C

-17- • Max working temperature at thermostat heat: 80˚C • Temperature differential (the differen ce between the opening, and closing temperature of the thermostat): 5˚C - 8˚C. • Manufacturing tolerance in opening, and closing temperature: low as possible. • Combined controller output contact rating (for fan operation): • Resistive: > 16 A • Inductive (full load ampere): > 5 A at p.f. 0.6 or 0.4 5- The controller housing should be prepared for wall mounting. 20. المواصفات الفنية لتوريد وتركيب نظـام إطفـاء تلقـائى بإستخدام الخرطوم الحساس بجهـاز إطـفاء ثانى أكسيد الكربون. الإستــخدام : يتتتتتتم إستتتتتتخدام ذلتتتتتك النظتتتتتـام المعتمتتتتتد علتتتتتى ختتتتتراطيم الإستشتتتتتعار الحساستتتتتة وذلتتتتتك لتغطيتتتتتة الأمتتتتتـاكن الضتتتتتيقة

11. أن يكونالبدن مدهون من الخارج بدهان الكتروستاتيك بالأحمر اللامع ، ومعالج من الداخل ضد الصدأ ( فسفته) . 12. أن يحقق التفريغ للمحتويات90% بدون تجميد 13. يجب أن يعالج بدن الجهاز بالوسائل المناسبة والكفيلة بإزالة أية زيت أو شحوم أو مواد عازلة قبل الطلاء وبما يوفر درجة عاليه من مقاومة الصدأ والتماسك بطبقة الطلاء والتى يجب أن يتوفر فيها الجودة العالية ومقاومة العوامل الجوية والخدوش والصدمات

0 EDMS 02-302-1 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR LOW VOLTAGE AUTOMATIC POWER FACTOR CORRECTION PANEL (APFC) Issue: Aug. 2025 / Rev- 1 • This revision contains option items that must be selected by EDC before bidding.

4 1. SCOPE This specification covers the minimum technical requirements for De sign, Engineering, Manufacture, testing at the manufacturer's factory, Site testing and Commissioning of Automatic Power Factor Correction (APFC) panels intended for use with low -voltage equipment to improve power factor and /or m itigate harmonics in AC electrical power systems, this specification is in addition to the special requirements of the Distribution Company. 2. APPLICABLE STANDARDS This specification shall be read in conjunction with latest revisions of the following EDMS specifications: Table (1) No. Standard No. Description 1 EDMS19-303 Low voltage circuit breakers 2 EDMS19-300 L.V H.R.C. Fuses LINK 3 EDMS19-301 L.V H.R.C. Fuses BASE

4 EDMS17-300 Low voltage current transformers 5 EDMS21-301 Low voltage capacitor 6 EDMS06-300 ECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR SINGLE CORE PVC-INSULATED FLEXIBLE NON SHEATHED CABLES (WIRES) Unless otherwise specified in this specification should be comply with the latest edition of IEC standard and should be designed, manufactured and tested in accordance to the latest applicable IEC standards as follows: No. Standard No. Description 1 IEC 61921 Power capacitors-low –voltage power factor correction banks 2 IEC 60831-1&2 Capacitor units and capacitor banks intended to be used, Particularly, for power- factor correction of A.C power Systems having a rated voltage up to and including 1000 V and frequencies 50 Hz. Guidelines for performance, rating

and testing. Safety requirements, Guide for installation and operation. 3 IEC 60529 Classification of degree of protection provided by enclosures. 4 IEC 61557-1 Electrical safety in low voltage distribution systems up to 1000VAC. 5 IEC 60947-1 Low-voltage switchgear and control gear. part 1 :general rules 6 IEC 60947-2 Low-voltage switchgear and control gear. Part 2 : circuit breakers 7 IEC 60947-4-1 Low-voltage switchgear and control gear. Part 4-1 : contactors and motor starters. 8 ISO 2063 Metallic coatings-protection of iron and steel against corrosion- metal Spraying of zinc and aluminum. 9 IEC 60439-1 Low-voltage switchgear and control gear assemblies-type tested and Partially type tested assemblies.
10 IEEE 18 Standard for Shunt Capacitor. 11 IEEE 519 Application of Harmonic Limits.

5 12 IEC 60076-6 Power Transformer reactor. 13 IEC 60085-1 Electrical insulation – thermal evaluation and designation 14 IEC 60664-1 Insulation coordination for equipment within low voltage systems 15 IEC 61326 Electrical equipment for measurement, control and laboratory use – EMC requirements 16 IEC 61439-1 Low voltage switchgear and control gear assemblies part 1:general rules 17 IEC 62208 Empty enclosures for low voltage switchgear and control gear assemblies – general requirements 18 IEC 62262 Degrees of protection provided by enclosures for electrical equipment against external mechanical impacts (IK code) 19 IEC 61000-6-4 Electromagnetic compatibility – generic standards – emission standard for

industrial environments 20 IEC 61000-6-2 Generic standards – immunity for industrial environments 21 IEC61642 Industrial a.c. networks affected by harmonics-application of filters and shunt capacitors 3. ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS The electrical and mechanical properties of the required APFC panel should be guaranteed under the following environmental conditions: Table (2) Annual average ambient temperature 35 ºC Ambient temperature -5°C to +55°C (class D) Average relative humidity 95 % over a period of 24 h and 90 % over a period of one month. Altitude Up to 1000 m above sea-level 4. GENERAL TECHNICAL DATA The required APFC panel should have a safe behavior and meet all requirements given below:

Table (3) System voltage 400 V Nominal operating (Service) voltage 380 V Rated frequency 50 Hz. IP class for Enclosure IP 41 (for indoor) IP 54 ( for outdoor) 5. CONSTRUCTION REQUIREMENTS 1. The Design and Component Selection of the Automatic Power Factor Correction (APFC) system shall consider continuous operation at maximum withstand voltage of 440/480/525 V(as option) and ensure reliable performance in consideration of room ambient temperature of 50°C. 2. The APFC components shall be of Type Tested according to IEC relevant. 3. Component Withstand Capability: All components shall be able to withstand dynamic, thermal, and dielectric stresses resulting from prospective short-circuit currents without

6 4. Switching Device Requirement: all capacitor banks shall be provided with a switching device equipped with an automatic trip. 5. Harmonic Current Consideration: Due to the presence of harmonic currents and manufacturing tolerances, components (MCCB, BUSBAR, and POWER CABLES) shall be designed for 1.5 times the nominal current according to the EGYPTAIN CODE. 6. Mounting Arrangement of Capacitors : shall allow for visual inspection and maintenance access while allowing an individual capacitor to be removed without disconnection or removal of any adjacent capacitors. 7. Capacitor Mounting Flexibility: Power capacitors shall also be capable of being mounted in both vertical and horizontal positions.

8. Installation Location: The APFC equipment shall be installed in cool, ventilated locations away from other heat-radiating elements. 9. Protection Against Environmental Factors: Adequate protection shall be provided against dust and direct solar radiation. 10. Ventilation System: A ventilation fan and air inlet filter unit shall be provided for the capacitor banks to facilitate better heat dissipation, the capacitors shall be placed to ensure adequate heat dissipation by convection and radiation 11. Thermostat Operation: The ventilation fan shall be operated by two thermostats set at 35°C and 55°C, respectively. (adjustable) 12. Temperature Rise Calculations: Temperature rise calculations shall be submitted along with

the offer to prove that the temperature rise is within the temperature tolerance levels of the capacitor bank components. 13. Temperature Rise Test: The mounting of PFC components and their suitability shall be revalidated by a temperature rise calculation as per IEC 61921considering min air flow available. 5.1 ENCLOSURE 5.1.1 General Requirements 1. The APFC capacitor bank shall be housed in a suitable metallic enclosure with a front door and provision for locking arrangements. 2. The dimensions of the APFC enclosures shall comply with the size of the installed components and according to technical industrial standards. 3. The APFC enclosure design shall ensure safe and reliable operation in both indoor and outdoor

applications. 4. The enclosure shall be designed to give maximum reliability in service operation, inspection, and maintenance. 5. The APFC enclosure shall be of frame construction made from sheets of cold or hot rolled steel (galvanized steel optional according to EDC requirements). 6. The APFC enclosure shall comply with standard IEC 60529. 7. The APFC enclosure doors shall be provided with vertical metal support or any other suitable supporting method accepted by EDC. 8. Where necessary, ribs/reinforcements shall be used to prevent excessive wobbling, especially on

7 the doors. 9. Welded structures and fabricated components shall be pretreated and then electrostatic powder coated to provide protection against environmental factors. 10. The front doors of the enclosure shall be installed using galvanized metal or stainless steel hinges (at least three hinges per door) except the type tested enclosure. 11. The doors shall be locked with a nickel-plated lock with a master key and rod to close the panel's door at three points except the type tested enclosure. 12. The enclosure door shall be provided with suitable gaskets. 13. All enclosures or partitions, including locking means and hinges for doors, shall be of mechanical strength sufficient to withstand stresses during normal service and short-circuit conditions.

5.1.2 OUTDOOR INSTALLATION 1. Roof Enclosure: The roof enclosure shall have a pyramidal slope on sides or back of 6 degrees with the horizontal. 2. Enclosure Material: The enclosure shall be made from cold or hot rolled steel with a thickness of not less than 2.5 mm before painting (or IK10 supported with certificate) (or galvanized steel optional according to EDC requirements). 3. Painting: The paint shall be exterior painting. 4. Electrostatic Powder Coating: The enclosure shall be coated with electrostatic powder with a thickness of 60-80 microns everywhere. 5. Protection Degree: The protection degree shall be not less than IP54. 5.1.3 INDOOR INSTALLATION 1. Enclosure Material: The enclosure shall be made from cold or hot rolled steel with a thickness

of not less than 2 mm before painting (or galvanized steel optional according to EDC requirements). 2. Painting: The enclosure shall be provided with primary and finishing coat of electrostatic painting powder on the inside and outside. The external coat is preferred to be high gray color (RAL 7035). 3. Rust Treatment: The enclosure shall be treated against rust. 4. Electrostatic Powder Coating: The enclosure shall be coated with electrostatic powder with a thickness of 60-80 microns everywhere. 5. Gaskets: The enclosure door shall be provided with suitable gaskets. 6. Protection Degree: The protection degree shall be not less than IP41. 7. Lifting Eye-Bolts: The whole unit enclosure shall have two eye -bolts diagonals to be lifted

and raising plinth. 5.2 BUS-BARS 1. Material and Purity: All bus-bars shall be made of rectangular, high-grade electrolytic copper with a minimum purity of 99.99% (conductivity: 57 MS/m). A certificate from a neutral and accredited body shall be provided, and the distribution company reserves the right to take a

8 random sample for analysis during inspection and testing at the supplier's expense. 2. The maximum current density of bus-bar shall be 1.5 A/mm2 except the type tested panel at 45°C. 3. Insulation: All bus-bars shall be insulated with colored, heat-shrinkable, non-flammable, and non-hygroscopic tubes (red, yellow, blue, and black) for the 3 -phases and neutral bus -bars, respectively and the extension copper links for C.B (excluding type tested panels). 4. Fault Current Withstand: The bus-bar section of power factor assemblies shall withstand a minimum fault current of the system at the point of connection. 5. Arrangement and Assembly: Phase bus-bars shall be arranged systematically and assembled

using insulators. 6. Cross-Sectional Area: • Neutral bus-bar: 100% of the CSA of the phase bus-bars. • Earth bus-bar: at least 25% of the CSA of the phase bus-bars. 7. Mounting and Insulators : Bus-bars shall be mounted on insulators made from porcelain or cast resin according to IEC standards. 8. Short-Circuit Current Withstand : Bus -bars and insulators shall withstand thermal and mechanical effects resulting from short-circuit currents according to IEC 61439-1. 9. Neutral Bus -Bar Connections : The neutral bus -bar shall be equipped wi th necessary compression lugs (Bimetal, Al-Cu) suitable for connection to aluminum neutral conductors and PVC-insulated cables (concerned by the contractor).

5.3 CAPACITOR BANKS 5.3.1 General Requirements - Modular Construction: The APFC capacitor banks shall be of modular construction and shall be switched ON/OFF automatically in steps through the use of capacitor duty contactors. 5.3.2 Components 1. Main Incomer Isolating ACB/MCCB 2. HRC Fuse (or MCCB as options): Each stage of the capacitor bank shall also have a suitable rated HRC fuse or MCCB as options. 3. Contactors: Three-pole contactors for capacitor switching for each stage. 4. Power Factor Regulator: Microprocessor-based power factor regulator for automatic power factor correction. 5. Capacitor Units : Power factor improvement three -phase capacitors, arranged in a suitable number of stages.
6. Detuning Reactors : Detuned three -phase iron -cored series reactors for harmonic current suppression and to prevent resonance (as options).

9 5.3.3 Design and Performance 1. Automatic Operation: The automatic capacitor bank should be ready for field connection with all components clearly labeled for identification. 2. Component Coordination : To ensure safety, reliability, and accountability of component coordination, all major capacitor bank components should be preferably from a single manufacturer. 3. Temperature Conditions: The capacitor bank shall be designed for trouble-free service under arduous temperature conditions. 5.4 CAPACITOR UNITS - The capacitor units specification shall comply with EDMS 21-301 5.5 Switching and protective devices and connections 5.5.1 Transient Overcurrent Withstand - The switching and protective devices and connections shall be capable of withstanding

electrodynamic and thermal stresses caused by transient overcurrent of high amplitude and frequency which occurs during switching on. 5.5.2 Cable and Circuitry Selection - The connecting cables and circuitry shall be selected to handle 1.5 times the rated capacitor current permanently. 5.5.3 Protection Requirements 1. The capacitor bank shall be protected by a suitable rated MCCB/ACB at the incomer. 2. HRC Fuses (MCCBs as option) shall be used for short-circuit protection. 3. HRC Fuses do not generally provide suitable overcurrent protection. 5.5.4 Circuit Breaker Specifications 1. The MCCB OR ACB shall comply with EDMS 19-303 2. The current rating of the MCCB shall be at least 1.5 times the full load current of the capacitor

bank. 5.5.5 Fuse and its Base Specifications 1. The HRC Fuses shall comply with EDMS 19-300 2. The fuses base shall comply with EDMS 19-301 3. The HRC fuse rating shall be (1.5) times the nominal capacitor current. 5.5.6 CAPACITOR DUTY CONTACTORS 1. Capacitor duty contactors is Specially designed for switching capacitors and provided with damping resistors which limit the value of inrush current to a save value. 2. The Capacitor duty contactors shall isolate all three supply phases to the capacitor on switch off. 3. The capacitor duty contactors are rated based on nominal reactive power. 4. The rated voltage of Capacitor duty contactors shall be equal to or higher than the maximum network voltage with the power factor correction installation.
5. The Capacitor duty contactors shall be designed for continuous current (including harmonics) which can pass the power factor correction installation and / or filter at maximum frequency and

10 extreme tolerance of the components, epically capacitors and reactors. 5.5.6.1 Contactors specifications (stage switching) 1. The switching contactor specifications shall comply with IEC 60947-4-1 2. The Capacitor duty contactors shall be three poles type and shall be employed for switching ON and switching OFF operations in capacitor banks. 3. Degree of protection IP IP20 Storage temperature °C -60°C:80°C operation temperature °C ≥ 55°C Humidity % up to 95% (non-condensing) Rated Impulse Withstand Voltage KV 8 KV 5.6 FILTER, DETUNING REACTORS ASSOCIATED WITH CAPACITORS (as option) 5.6.1 General 1. Filter reactors shall be used in series with the capacitors for cases where resonance is most likely to occur, this arrangement will ensure the reduction of harmonic distortion and avoidance of series / parallel resonance that can lead to: • Overloading of capacitors • Overloading of transformer and transmission equipment • Interference with metering and control system • Resonance elevation i.e. amplification of harmonics • Voltage distortion

2. Capacitors shall be positioned so as to avoid the occurrence of resonance, the same value of KVAR installed at high voltage rather than low voltage can eliminate a resonant condition. 3. This clause describes the requirements for reactors designed to be used in association with capacitors. 4. On main supplies with high level of harmonic pollution, it is recommended to use capacitor banks with detuned reactors which have the following functions: • Increasing the capacitor impedance in relation to the harmonic currents. • Shifting the parallel resonance frequency of the source and the capacitor to below the main frequencies of the harmonic currents that are causing interference.

11 • Filter reactors connected in series or in parallel with capacitors to reduce or block harmonics or control signals (ripple signals). 5.6.2 Detuned filtering (PFC-DF) 1. (Depending on the distribution company requirement: according to load analysis) 2. Capacitors with detuned filtering technique shall be employed to correct power factor while avoiding the risk of resonance condition. 3. This shall be performed by shifting the resonance frequency to lower values where no harmonic currents are present, by introducing a filter reactor in series with the capacitors, such that the capacitor/reactor combination is inductive at the dangerous frequencies but capacitive at fundamental frequency. 4. The circuit should be tuned such that the series resonant frequency should be below the lowest

harmonic order expected to be present in the electrical network. 5. The detuning reactors shall be connected in series with each capacitor stage and shall be of iron cored type. 6. The detuned reactor should be equipped with thermal switch inside the winding which should cut-off the respective contactor in case of over-temperature. 7. The reactors shall be selected taking into account the inductance value of the feeding line so as to get the desired tuning frequency and the current capability high enough for the harmonic current absorption that can be expected. 8. Filter reactors shall be designed to give an inductive voltage drop up to 10% of the system nominal voltage, thus protecting the capacitors from harmonics.

5.6.3 DETUNING REACTORS SPECIFICATIONS 1. The detuned reactors should be suitable to capacitor banks according to design. 2. The detuning reactor shall be manufactured in full compliance with and tested to the requirements of IEC 60076-6 Reactors. 3. The reactor insulation shall be class "F" or above 4. The maximum temperature of the reactor at maximum continuous RMS shall be no higher than 145 °C at a 50°C ambient. 5. If the lowest harmonic order expected to be present in the electrical network is likely to the 150 HZ (3 rd harmonics) then the series detuning reactors should be tuned for 135HZ 2.7 tuning order) with a relative impedance 14%. 6. Tolerance of reactors shall not exceed ±3% of rated value.

7. IEC 60289, IEC 60076-6, VDE 0532 8. Temperature class F 155°C (temperature rise limit at coils on air) the classification of insulations is that given in clause 2 of IEC 60085 9. Temperature protection 130 °C ± 5 % 10. The reactor shall be securely fastened and installed in a separate compartment of the power factor correction board, if necessary rubber pad shall be used to reduce noise.

thermal class (for dry type) – total mass – type of cooling. 5.7 POWER FACTOR CONTROLLER (Regulator) 5.7.1 General Requirements 1. The PFC controller is the brain of the APFC system, switching ON/OFF steps based on the KVAR required to maintain the power factor close to unity. 2. The regulator shall automatically and/or manually step-in or step-out various capacitors to correct the power factor to the required pre-set level.

13 3. The regulator shall be insensitive to harmonics and screened from stray electric and magnetic interference. 4. The regulator shall be housed in a case suitable for panel mounting. 5. Operating temperature shall be -5°C to +70°C. 6. The regulator shall be suitable for 1A or 5A current input. 7. The regulator (controller) has IP 42 as minimum. 5.7.2 Technical Requirements 1. The regulator shall be 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, or 14 steps type as specified in the drawing and/or Bill of Quantities. 2. No voltage trip and zero current trip shall be provided. 3. For a voltage loss of longer than 15ms or current loss of longer than 3s, all capacitor stages connected shall be switched off until discharged, after voltage or current is stored the regulator

switches the required steps on. 4. The regulator shall have an LCD display (2x16 characters) to indicate harmonics, current, voltage, frequency, power factor, active and reactive powers. 5. The controller shall have LED/LCD display. Parameter in display should be cyclic with suitable time delay. 6. All capacitor units (except fixed unit) should be controlled by a APFC controller, responsible for monitoring the reactive and active components of the network through the current and the voltage path. 7. The capacitor stages shall be switched in and out depending on the deviations of the actual power factor from the set value as calculated by the controller. 8. The controller shall sense current from a single phase and voltage from two phases to compute the

average 3-phase power factor and accordingly compensate by switching in necessary capacitor stages. 5.7.3 Functional Requirements 1. The regulator shall automatically program and control the capacitor bank. 2. The regulator shall sense the reactive current requirement and switch on/off the required stages of the capacitor bank. 3. The regulator shall maintain a targeted power factor within 0.95 lagging and unity. 4. The regulator shall be microprocessor-based. 5. The regulator shall detect any stage size by automatic recognition. 6. The regulator shall have a minimum time delay of 120 seconds for switching on a capacitor. 7. The regulator shall be panel-mounted and easily programmable. 8. The regulator shall conform to safety guidelines as per IEC 61010-1-2001.

9. The regulator shall have programmable dual cos phi. 10. The display unit shall be easily visible, preferably without opening the APFC cabinet door/cover. 11. The operating voltage shall be 380 volt / 220 volt so the controller can be connected 3 phases or 1 phase. 12. It can indicate the odd harmonics for voltage (as option). 13. It can switch off the stages when voltage harmonics exceeds the limits with IEEE519(as option).

14 14. It can display actual power factor and target power factor, active current, reactive current, the number of stages in service. 15. It can store the data in spite of the supply with switch off. 16. It can be operated in different programs. 17. It shall be flush mounted for indoor panels, and it shall be surface mounted for outdoor. 18. Regulator (controller) should have a possibility for downloading data through PC (as option). 19. The voltage on the capacitor terminals may be particularly high at times of light load conditions.in such cases, some or all of the capacitors shall be switched out of circuit in order to prevent overstressing of the capacitors and under voltage increase in the distribution system.

20. The controller shall be easily programmable from front panel itself. 21. The automatic power factor controller shall be manufactured in full compliance with and tested to the requirements of IEC 61326-1 (ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT FOR MEASUREMENT, CONTROL AND LABORATORY USE – EMC REQUIRMENTS – PART 1 : GENERAL REQUIREMENTS) 22. The capacitor bank and controller shall ensure that after the loss by fault of any one stage, it shall continue to operate automatically and shall follow rotational switching. 23. The threshold value for the operating temperature and system harmonics shall be programmable and the regulator shall be able to switch off the connected capacitor stages if the actual values exceed these thresholds.

24. Dual Cos Phi – the controller should have programmable dual cos phi to differentiate the need in compensation (Cos Phi) when the operating condition changes (as option). 5.7.4 SETTING AND DISPLAY 5.7.4.1 The power factor controller / regulator shall be incorporated the following setting: 1. It has the capability to adjust setting time of contact between stages 2. Recognize the connection of CT and be able to automatically adjust itself to the phase angle difference. 3. Regulator shall have the capability to automatically search and set the C/K setting, it shall be also possible to program the C/K setting manually. 4. C/K inductive setting and display 5. C/K capacitive setting and display

6. Target power factor setting and display (0.8 inductive-1-0.8 capacitive) 7. Operation delay time setting and display (5 s to 300s) 5.7.4.2 The power factor controller / regulator should allow the following readings: 1. Automatic initialization and stage rating detection 2. Any step sequence detection (user definable step sequence) 3. Measurement of capacitance per stage 4. Cap bank over load current ration 5. THD Voltage 6. 4 quadrant operation 7. Record of the Max temp internal of the capacitor bank since reset 8. System Voltage (V AC)

15 9. Temperature (°C) 10. Real time Cos phi 11. KVAR value to target Cos Phi 5.7.4.3 The controller shall initiate alarms and warnings in the following events. 1. Temperature limit is exceeded 2. Insufficient capacitor output 3. Overload current ratio limit is exceeded 4. THDV limit is exceeded (as option) 5. Low power factor / under compensation 6. Over compensation 7. Over current 8. Capacitor step defective 9. Over voltage 10. Total voltage harmonic distortion THDV 5.7.5 Mode of operation 5.7.5.1 Manual operation mode - The capacitor steps can be switched in step by step and switched out in reverse step by step manually, and the step that is being switched on shall be displayed. 5.7.5.2 automatic operation mode

- The regulator shall switch on or switch out the required capacitors without undue switching on and switching off at any particular step to reach the nominal target power factor. 5.7.5.3 OFF mode - All equipment shall be switched off and de-energized. 5.8 CURRENT TRANSFORMERS - The current transformer shall comply with EDMS 17-300 and shall be specified according to KVAR panel rating 5.9 NAME PLATE, SAFETY INSTRUCTORS 1. • Tests on auxiliary and control circuit 5.13.1.4 Reactor unit tests • Measuring winding resistance. • Measuring od impedance at rated continuous current. • Measuring of losses at ambient temperature. • Winding of over voltage test. 5.13.2 TYPE TESTS 1. • Thermal stability test • Measurement of (tan Ϭ) of a capacitor. • AC test between terminals and container.

Insulation resistance test shall be carried out at all main circuits through to final terminal. 2. Insulation resistance shall exceed 10 MΩ. 3. Record all measurements. 4. operation. • Insulation resistance tests between phases and earth and between neutral and earth. • Operational test on components. • Switching ON/OFF of capacitor bank on various KVAR requirement. • Checking of display parameters • Switching on/off logic verification • Data communication through serial / optical port • Verification of data / reports / functions in base computer software. 5.13.6 Final inspection 1. • Insulation resistance measurements on the buses, phase to phase and phase to ground, with all breakers in the fully connected position and contacts open. • Control circuit insulation resistance to ground. • Inspection all relays and protective devices, and verify setting in accordance with the manufacturer’s instructions. Inspect current transformers and relays for correct polarity of connections and the installation of jumpers on unused current transformer circuits. • Manually close and trip each breaker checking and adjusting the main contact alignment and wiring action in accordance with the manufacturer’s instructions. • Test protective relay operation for incomer air circuit breakers.

Page 3 of 12 1. SCOPE: This specification covers the minimum technical requirements for design, engineering, manufacturing, testing and packing of the three phase low voltage circuit breakers to be used in low voltage compartment of three phase distribution kiosk and three phase distribution cabinets in the LV distribution system. 2. APPLICABLE STANDARDS: Unless otherwise specified in this specification should be com ply with the latest edition of IEC standard and should be designed, manufactured and tested in accordance to the latest applicable IEC standards as follows: No. Standard Description 1 IEC60947-1 Low Voltage Switchgear and Control gear General Rules. 2 IEC 60947-2 Low Voltage Switchgear and Control gear for Circuit Breakers

3 IEEEC37.13 For De-rating 3. ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITION The performance of low voltage assembly (panel) should be guaranteed for following environmental conditions, any differences in the guaranteed performance should be clearly set out in the offer: Ambient temperature -5°C to +45°C (50°C is an option according to --EDC requirements), Maximum relative humidity 95 % Altitude Up to 1000 m above mean sea-level 4. DESIGN CRITERIA 1- Required rated current of circuit breaker at 40°C (Optional at 50°C according to –EDC requirements) 2- In case of the required rated current of circuit breaker at 50 oC and the tested one at 40 oC, should be apply derating guide table 3- Circuits Breakers described in this specification shall be suitable for operation in --EDC system
parameters and service conditions. 4- Circuit breakers should be fixed Type, Indoor, Three-pole, for surface or panel mounting.

Page 4 of 12 5- Air circuit Breakers with electronic control shall be equipped with electronic solid state trip - control unit for overload and short circuit protection with sealing provision, (Optional for MCCB CBs). 6- The circuit breaker shall have instantaneous short circuit tripping characteristics such that the fault will trip the circuit breaker in a fraction of a second. 7- Air circuit breakers shall be with inverse time delay thermal and instantaneous short circuit release on all poles of the circuit breakers 8- The circuit breakers shall have on, off and trip position 9- The circuit breakers shall be hand operated through handle or as required. 10- The insulating materials used shall be non-flammable and non-hygroscopic.

11- The conductive parts of circuit breakers should be made of high grade copper silver coated. 12- The time-current characteristics of releases should be provided with the offer. 13- All circuit breakers should be fitted with test facility which allows the release test to be done; this test must be carried out with circuit breaker closed and with no current. 14- All circuit breakers should be suitable for isolation. 15- All MCCB circuit breakers shall be supplied with 3 extension copper bars (or 6 bars) of vertical length not more than 7 cm with current density not more than 1.5 A/mm2 and the middle bar must be in different level than other adjacent bars.(as option) 16- Withdrawable air Circuit Breaker ( as option )

a. The withdrawable mechanism shall be fitted with a reliable indicating device which indicates unambiguously the positions of the isolating contacts. b. The withdrawable mechanism shall be fitted with interlocks which only permit the isolating contacts to be separated or reclosed when th e main contacts of the circuit - breaker are open. c. In addition, the withdrawable mechanism shall be fitted with interlocks which only permit the main contacts to be closed • when the isolating contacts are fully closed, or • when the specified isolating distance is achieved between the fixed and moving parts of the isolating contacts (disconnected position). d. Should be include a mechanical position indication with an acknowledge facility.

e. Safety shutters should be available so that it prevents the direct contact with live parts. 5. INSPECTION AND TESTING: 1) --EDC Company for Electricity Distribution preserves its right to witness the routine testing of circuit breakers.

Page 5 of 12 2) Unless otherwise specified or approved in writing by (--EDC) all circuit breakers shall be tested in accordance with all relevant latest applicable IEC standards and test report certificate shall be provided with it. (--EDC) may carryout testing at manufacturer / supplier lab. The breaker will be assumed as rejected if any functions of the breakers are found faulty. 5.1 TYPE TEST 2) Type Test certificate according to the latest IEC 60947-2 and shall include the following type tests: - Short -circuit breaking capacity - Temperature rise. - Overload performance (Circuit breakers of rated up to and including 630 A ). - Short-time withstand current - Dielectric properties. - Tripping limits and characteristics.

- Operational performance capability. 5.2 ROUTINE TEST 1) All routine tests prescribed in the relevant IEC 60947-2 standard shall be carried out by the manufacturer / supplier on his expense and under the full control and supervision of (--EDC) witness engineers. 2) The routine test samples will be randomly selected by (--EDC) from each offered lot of the same type and rating. The test will be done at an approved testing laboratory or manufacturer /supplier laboratory as will be mutually agreed upon. 3) The routine test will include the following: - Mechanical operation tests (No-load). - Verification of the Calibration of over-current releases (shall be test phase by phase). - Verification of the operation of under voltage and shunt releases.

- Dielectric tests. - Verification of clearances. 5.3 SAMPLE ( ACCEPTANCE )TEST:- • --EDC has right to make AM routine test on random samples for each shipment of delivery • No. of samples shall be decided by –EDC at tender phase.

Page 6 of 12 6. PACKING & SHIPPING The packing shall be as following: - Each MCCB/ACB and its accessories shall be separately packed as a complete unit/assembly and shall be delivered ready for services. - Packing shall be protected against damage during shipment and handling to installation site. - Packing openings shall be closed to prevent entry of dust, dirt and other foreign matters. - Packing shall be marked with following: • Manufacturer's name • Country of origin • Weight in kilogram 7. MARKING The following data shall be marked on the Circuit Breaker itself or on a nameplate engraved or laser printed on the circuit breaker, and located in a place such that they are visible and legible

when circuit breaker is installed: - Manufacturer name - Country of origin - Circuit breaker type - Serial number - Year of manufacture - No of IEC standard - Rated current (In) at --°C (if more than 40°C) - Rated voltage (Vn) - Rated thermal (Ith) and magnetic (Im) current - Rated frequency. - Rated service short-circuits breaking capacity (Ics) at 400v. - Rated ultimate short-circuit breaking capacity (Icu) at 400v.

Page 7 of 12 8. SUBMITTALS a. Vendor shall provide the following with the quotation: 1. Shall be fill in thoroughly the attached guarantee tables. 2. Technical catalogues containing the technical data of circuit breakers 3. Installation and maintenance instructions. 4. Copy of type test certificate for circuit breaker. 5. Submitted a valid approved letter from the Egyptian Electricity Holding Company. 6. Submit with the offer sample of each item ( optional according to –EDC) b. Vendor shall provide the following with delivering: 1. Operation (user) manual. 9. GUARANTEE • The supplier guarantees the circuit breaker against all defects arising out of faulty design or workmanship, or of defective material for period of 18 months from date of delivery or 12

months from date of operation whichever comes first. • Guarantee period shall be duplicated for circuit breaker which deliver for first time. • The good quality and manufacturing of the materials should be guaranteed. • Any parts which on account of poor quality of the materials, manufacturing defects or poor assembly, should be replaced or repaired in the shortest time possible and free of charge.

1 IEC61439-1 Low-voltage switchgear and controlgear assemblies 2 IEC60947 Low-voltage switchgear and controlgear rules. 3 IEC 62262 Degrees of protection provided by enclosures for electrical equipment against external mechanical impacts (IK code) 4 IEC60529 Classification of degree of protection provided by Enclosures 3. الاشتراطات البيئية يجب ضمان أداء لوحة الجهد المنخفض ان تتوافق مع الظروف البيئية الموضحة ادناه ، ويجب توضيح أي اختلافات في الأداء بوضوح في العرض المقدم: 1. SCOPE - These specifications cover the design, manufacturing, assembly (panel), and supply of 50 c/s, 0.4KV low voltage distribution panels. - The distribution panels should be connected to 0.4 KV solidly-earthed networks and should be fed from

distribution transformers with variable rating up to 4000 KVA according to the load. 2. APPLICABLE STANDARDS This specification sha ll be read in conjunction with latest revisions of the following EDMS specifications: No. Standard Description 1 EDMS 19-300 L.V Fuse Link 2 EDMS-19-302 L.V 630 A Switch-fuse 3 EDMS-19-303 L.V Circuit Breakers Unless otherwise specified in this specifi cation should be comply with the latest edition of IEC standard and should be designed, manufactured and tested in accordance to the latest applicable IEC standards as follows: No. Standard Description 1 IEC61439-1 Low-voltage switchgear and controlgear assemblies 2 IEC 60947 Low-voltage switchgear and controlgear rules.

3 IEC 62262 Degrees of protection provided by enclosures for electrical equipment against external mechanical impacts (IK code) 4 IEC60529 Classification of degree of protection provided by Enclosures 3. DISTRIBUTION PANELS FOR TRANSFORMERS UP TO 4000KVA pg. 4 Ambient temperature -5°C to +45°C (50°C is an option according to -- EDC requirements), Maximum relative humidity 95 % Altitude Up to 1000 m above mean sea-level 4. متطلبات عامة 1. تصنع اللوحة بأبعاد مناسبة طبقا لما يتطلبه حجم المهمات المركبة داخلها وحسب الأصول الفنية للصناعة. 2. تصنع اللوحة من ألواح الصلب المسحوب على البارد /الساخن (تكون مجلفنة اختياري طبقا لمتطلبات شركات التوزيع) بسمك لا يقل عن 2مم قبل الدهان والأبواب مزودة بع صب طولي من الداخل (أو ما يحقق التدعيم الكامل للابواب بطريقة أخري مقبولة لشركات التوزيع) يستثنى من ذلك اللوحات المختبرة نوعيا. 3. تبنى اللوحة على هيكل من الدعامات بسمك لا يقل عن 2.5مم قبل الدهان (او مجلفنة يستثنى من ذلك اللوحات

المختبرة نوعيا واللوحات المعلقة حيث يتم تركيب جميع أجزاء ومكونات اللوحة عليه. 4. يجب عمل مرايا /ابواب مقسمة للخلية بالكامل و على القواطع والسويتش فيوز و دخول وخروج الكابلات بحيث لا تظهر الا ايدي التشغيل وتصنع من الصاج المسحوب على البارد/ الساخن بسمك لا يقل عن 1.25مم قبل الدهان. 5. تدهن اللوحة ببودرة الكتروستاتيك المقاوم للصدأ. 6. يجب ان تزود اللوحة بوسيلة لحمل اللوحة تكفي لرفع 1.25 وزن اللوحة. 7. تكون اللوحة محكمة الغلق ومجهزة ضد العوامل الجوية وتكون ذات درجة الحماية لا تقل عن (IP41) في حالة التركيب داخل المبني. 8. في حالة ان تكون اللوحة خارج المبنى، يجب مراعاة الآتي: • الا تقل درجة حماية اللوحة عنIP54. • ان يكون سقف اللوحة مائل (هرميمائل على الاجناب او الى الخلف) بزاوية ميل لا تقل عن 6 درجات مع

الافقي. • الا يقل سمك الصاجالمسحوب على الساخن/البارد عن 2.5مم قبل الدهان او اختبار الصدمات الميكانيكية لا يقل ع ن IK10 (يمكن ان يكون الصاج مجلفن طبقا لطلب شركة التوزيع كبند اختياري) • ان يكون الدهان مقاوم للظروف خارج المبنى. Ambient temperature -5°C to +45°C (50°C is an option according to -- EDC requirements), Maximum relative humidity 95 % Altitude Up to 1000 m above mean sea-level 4. GENERAL REQUIREMENT 1. The dimensions of the assembly ( panel) should comply with the size of the installed components and according to the technical industrial standards. 2. The panel should be made from sheets of cold / hot rolled steel (galvanize d steel optional according to …EDC requirements) with a thickness of at least 2

mm (before painting) and the doors should be provided with vertical metal support (Or any other suitable supporting method accepted by …EDC) excluding type tested panels. 3. The panel shall be having frame structure not less than 2.5mm before painting (or galvanized) excluding type tested panel s, and pole -mounted panels where all panel`s parts and component are fixed on this structure. 4. The front cover /compartimized doors shall be made for all cubical, and circuit breakers and switch-fuse by cold/hot rolled steel sheets with thickness not less than 1.25mm. 5. The panel should be painted rust-resistant electrostatic powder painting. 6. The panel shall be provided with lifting means to achieve a weight of 1.25 times panel weight.

7. The panel should be firmly locked and weather -proof with a degree of protection "IP41" for indoor. 8. Incase of outdoor panels , the following shall be take n in consideration: • Panel protection degree is not less than IP 54. • Roof panel shall be having slope (pyramidal slope on sides or back) by 6° with horizontal. • Panel will be made from cold/ hot rolled steel sheet with thickness not less than 2.5mm before painting (or IK 10 supported with certificate) (or galvanized steel according to –EDC requirement optional). • Paint shall be exterior painting.

DISTRIBUTION PANELS FOR TRANSFORMERS UP TO 4000KVA pg. 5 • او يتم عمل لوحة داخل المبنى ثم يتم وضعها في حاوية من الصاج سمك 2.5 مم قبل الدهان اوتقديم شهادة اختبار الصدمات الميكانيكية لا يقل عن IK10 وذات سقف مائل بدرجة حماية لا تقل عن IP54 (بند اختياري). • في حالة اللوحات المعلقة يجب تجهيز اللوحة بدعامات من الخلف ومن القاعدة لإمكانية تثبيتها على العامود. 9. تركب الأبواب الأمامية للوحة بواسطة مفصلات من الحديد المجلفن او الاستانلس ستيل (ثلاث مفصلات لكل باب على الأقل) وتقفل الأبواب بقفل مطلي بالنيكل بمفتاح master key مزود بسباليونة لإغلاق الباب في ثلاث نقاط. 10. لا يتم اجراء اي عمليات ميكانيكية بعد الانتهاء من عملية الدهان. 5. المواصفات الفنية- 5-1 قضبان توصيل التيارالكهربائي: -

1. يجب ان تصنع جميع البارات في اللوحة من النحاس الإلكتروليتي المبطط مستطيل الشكل ذو درجة نقاوة لا تقل عن 99.99 % (التوصيلية لا تقل عن57 سيمنز (يجب تقديم شهادة من جهة محايدة ومعتمدة ويحق لشركة التوزيع أخذ عينة عشوائية أثناء الفحص والاختبار وذلك لتحليلها في احد المعامل المعتمدة على نفقة المورد). 2. يجب ان تكون جميع البارات الرئيسية (الأوجه الرئيسية والتعادل) معزولة بأنابيب انكماشية ملونة مقاومة للاشتعال ولا تمتص الرطوبة بالالوان (أحمر، أصفر، ازرق، اسود) على الترتيب وكذلك نزلات القواطع والسويتش فيوز النحاسية يستثنى من ذلك اللوحات المختبرة نوعيا. 3. يجبان تكون قواطع الخروج / السويتش فيوز على الترتيب في وضع رأسي (الدخول من اعلى والخروج من اسفل) او افقي طبقا لطلب شركة التوزيع. 4. يجب ان تكون مساحة مقطع القضبان الرئيسية والتعادل

طبقا لما هو موضح في جدول رقم (1) & (2) او طبقا لجداول المصنع في حالة اللوحات المختبرة نوعيا عند 45 درجة مئوية. 5. قضيب الأرضي يجب أن يكون ذو مساحة مقطع لا تقل عن 25% من مساحة مقطع بارات الاوجه. 5-2 قواطع التيار وسكاكين المصهرات- أ. بالنسبة للقاطع الرئيسي: تحتوي لوحة التوزيع علي قاطع رئيسي ثلاثي الأوجه (هوائي أو مقولب) قابل للضبط ضد زيادة التيار وتيار • Or manufacturing indoor panel and installed inside outdoor enclosure thickness of steel sheet is not less than 2.5 mm , or IK10 supported with certificate , IP54, and with slope roof)(optional). • In case of pole mounted panels, panel shall be equipped with facility (support on the back and the base) for easy and safe fixing on the pole 9. The front doors of the panel should be installed using

galvanized metal or stainless steel hinges (three hinges per each at least) and locked with a nickel -plated lock with a master key with rod to close panel`s door at three points. 10. It is not allowed to do any mechanical process after painting. 5. Technical Specification 5-1 Bus-bars: 1. All the bus -bars should be rectangular high -grade electrolytic copper with a purity of at least 99.99% (conductivity 57 SM/m) ( A certificate must b e presented from a neutral and accredited body, and the distribution company has the right to take a random sample during inspection and testing in order to analyze it in one of the approved laboratories at the supplier’s expense). 2. All the bus-bars should be insulated with colored heat-

shrinkable non-flammable and non -hygroscopic tubes (red, yellow, blue, and black) for the 3 -phas and neutral bus-bars, respectively and the extension cooper links for C.B and switch -fuse too (excluding type tested panels). 3. outgoing C.Bs . and switch -fuse installed vertical position (incoming from top and outgoing from bottom)/horizontal (according to –EDC requirements). 4. The cross -section area of all main B.B and neutral should be according to table (1) &(2) or according to manufacturer`s tables for type tested panels at 45C°. 5. The cross-section area of the earth bus -bar should be at least 25% of that of the phases bus-bars. 5.2 Circuit-Breakers and Switch fuses: A. For Main C.B.: -
The panel includes one main 3 -poles CB (ACB or MCCB) complete with overload current and short circuit current Protection and without under voltage
